events.description: Senator Murray introduced S. 716 to authorize issuance of patent in fee to George Peters for land on Crow Indian Reservation; bill read twice and referred to Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs
George Peters wrote letter to Senator Murray (referenced in Murray's January 26 reply)
Senator Murray replied to George Peters advising he had prepared and was introducing a new bill to authorize issuance of patent in fee
Department of the Interior (Oscar L. Chapman, Undersecretary) issued report on S. 716 recommending enactment with amendments to allow sale to a Crow Indian under existing regulations
Joseph C. O'Mahoney, Chairman of Senate Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs, wrote to Senator Murray attaching Department of Interior report on S. 716 and asking if amendments were agreeable
House Committee on Public Lands reported S. 716 favorably without amendment and recommended bill do pass; committed to Committee of the Whole House and ordered to be printed as Report No. 508

Senator Murray wrote to George Peters advising that S. 716 passed the Senate on April 11th and had been reported favorably out of House Committee on Public Lands; enclosed copy of House report; bill to come up on next private calendar
S. 716 passed the House and was cleared for President's signature
Senator Murray sent telegram (Day Letter) to George Peters at Box 34, Wyola, Montana, informing him that S. 716 passed the House and was cleared for President's signature
Senate Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs amended S. 716 at suggestion of Department of the Interior to permit Secretary to sell land under existing regulations rather than issue patent in fee
George Peters indicated desire to sell his land to George Redfield, another Crow Indian

financial_transactions.description: Proposed sale of 840 acres of George Peters' allotted land on Crow Indian Reservation to a Crow Indian under existing regulations, authorized by amended S. 716

correspondence.subject: Request regarding patent in fee for allotted land

correspondence.subject: Introduction of new bill to authorize patent in fee

correspondence.recipient_title: Chairman, Senate Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs
correspondence.subject: Department report on S. 716 authorizing patent in fee to George Peters
correspondence.action_requested: Amend S. 716 to authorize Secretary to sell land to a Crow Indian under existing regulations rather than issue patent in fee; amend title accordingly; require written application from George Peters

correspondence.action_requested: Advise committee whether amendments are agreeable
correspondence.outcome: Amendments were accepted; bill amended and passed Senate April 11, 1949
